attributes.PROJ_NAME: Fire Station No. 4 Phase II Remodel
attributes.REC_UPDATE: Construction is complete.
attributes.PROJ_DESC: This project provides for the repair of fire station deficiencies, including roofing repairs, HVAC cleaning, ceiling tile replacement, flooring, and wall repairs.

attributes.REC_UPDATE: Construction is complete.
attributes.PROJ_DESC: The project provides traffic signal upgrades at various corridors including the installation audible pedestrian countdown signal heads, ADA compliant audible pedestrian pushbutton system, and upgrading traffic signal controllers at various locations.

attributes.REC_UPDATE: Construction is complete.
attributes.PROJ_DESC: This project provides for improvements to the existing Civic Center Library Courtyard to better accommodate Library sponsored events.

This project provides for completion of a study to determine the necessary expansion(s) and assess future, long term relocation options for the Oceanside Police Department Headquarters. The Oceanside Police Department Improvements project includes improvements to the evidence room and property storage at the City Operation Center and the addition of a staff restroom.

attributes.PROJ_NAME: Beachfront Improvements Feasibility Study - Phase II
attributes.REC_UPDATE: The project is in the Environmental and Preliminary Design Phase. This phase is anticipated to be complete in Summer 2026.
attributes.PROJ_DESC: This project provides for preliminary design, environmental clearance and permitting for the Beachfront Improvement Phase II area. The Phase II area includes the Junior Seau Beach Community Center, Amphitheater/Bandshell, Pier Plaza, and public sections located in between. This project will advance the conceptual renderings to more detailed architectural and engineering project plans.

attributes.REC_UPDATE: All regulatory agency permits have been submitted as of December 2024. Staff is working with the various regulatory agencies to get permit approval.
attributes.PROJ_DESC: This federally funded project provides for the retrofit of Douglas Drive Bridge (over the San Luis Rey River) in order to meet earthquake standards.
attributes.LOC_DESC: Douglas Dr between Old River St & Pala Rd

attributes.REC_UPDATE: Preliminary design currently underway.
attributes.PROJ_DESC: The Ron Ortega Dog Facility scope includes development of up to 2.8 acres of vacant, unprogrammed, and under-utilized space designated in the diagram below. It is anticipated to include separate off-leash, fenced pens for large and small dogs to run and play freely, and will also include seating, shade structure(s), and drinking fountains for both dogs and humans. Improvements located outside of the fenced pens include lit, accessible sidewalks, as well as landscape and drainage improvements.

attributes.REC_UPDATE: Active work will be shown by various other CIP projects as funding is available
attributes.PROJ_DESC: This project brings portions of aging or deteriorating sidewalks and ramps into current Americans with Disabilities Act (ADA) compliance. The project provides for the installation or replacement of sidewalks and pedestrian ramps at various City-wide locations. There are two locations with completed construction and nearing construction. The first location is the Grandview Street Improvements Project in the Fire Mountain community. The second location is the City-wide Traffic Signal Curb Ramp Project. This project is funded in part by Measure X. ( https://www.ci.oceanside.ca.us/government/financial-services/measure-x-sales-tax )

attributes.PROJ_NAME: Oceanside Pier and Restaurant - Kiosk Demolition
attributes.REC_UPDATE: Demolition of the fire-impacted areas is complete. Efforts to reconstruct the hammerhead of the Pier are in progress.

attributes.PROJ_DESC:
The concrete bridge structure that spans Cassidy Street between Myers Street and 
Pacific Street was constructed in 1927, the same year as the concrete portion of the 
Oceanside Pier.  The bridge was closed to vehicle traffic in response to pieces of the 
bridge separating and falling onto Cassidy Street.  The bridge currently serves as a 
pedestrian only foot bridge and bicycle route.
attributes.LOC_DESC: Between Pacific Street and Myers Street
